data_layer_findings.finding: window.dataLayer exists in production after direct GA4 initializes.
data_layer_findings.evidence: Chrome CDP Runtime.evaluate on /, /contact, and /en returned dataLayer entries for js, config, gtm.dom, and gtm.load.

data_layer_findings.finding: gtm.dom, gtm.load, and gtm.historyChange-v2 entries exist even though no GTM container was loaded.
data_layer_findings.evidence: These entries are produced through the direct gtag/dataLayer mechanism and are not evidence of an active GTM container.

data_layer_findings.finding: No PII was observed in sampled production dataLayer entries.
data_layer_findings.evidence: Sampled initial and SPA dataLayer entries contained js/config/history/system_page_view style events only.

data_layer_findings.finding: Application event system_page_view was observed on system detail navigation and sent to direct GA4.
data_layer_findings.evidence: Chrome CDP network request to region1.google-analytics.com/g/collect with event system_page_view.

consent_findings.finding: No cookie/privacy consent UI was detected in production HTML or rendered DOM samples.
consent_findings.evidence: No common cookie/consent UI selectors or platform markers were detected on /, /rfq, or /en.

consent_findings.finding: No Consent Mode calls were detected.
consent_findings.evidence: Production HTML checks found zero gtag('consent'), analytics_storage, ad_storage, ad_user_data, or ad_personalization markers.

consent_findings.finding: Direct GA4 loads before any observed consent decision.
consent_findings.evidence: Chrome CDP observed gtag.js and GA4 page_view collection on initial page load; no consent UI or consent default/update events were observed.

csp_findings.finding: Production sends Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only, not enforced CSP.
csp_findings.evidence: Response headers include Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only with script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' and connect-src 'self'.

csp_findings.finding: If enforced as-is, current CSP would break active direct GA4.
csp_findings.evidence: Chrome console reported report-only violations for loading googletagmanager.com/gtag/js and connecting to region1.google-analytics.com/g/collect.

duplicate_findings.finding: No initial page-view duplication observed on sampled clean loads.
duplicate_findings.evidence: One GA4 page_view request each for /, /contact, and /en.

duplicate_findings.finding: No GTM/direct-GA4 duplicate source observed because GTM is not active in production.
duplicate_findings.evidence: No gtm.js requests in HTML or Chrome network.

duplicate_findings.finding: Potential RFQ conversion duplication remains in application code.
duplicate_findings.evidence: On successful RFQ, source code sends both rfq_submit_success and generate_lead through trackEvent; no production form submission was performed.

duplicate_findings.finding: SPA page views are missing on some client-side transitions.
duplicate_findings.evidence: homepage -> systems and contact -> RFQ produced zero GA4 page_view requests in Chrome CDP capture.

high if rfq_submit_success is also conversion

access_limitations: Vercel environment activation could not be verified from the local repository because the checkout is not linked to a Vercel project.
GTM internal container configuration could not be inspected because no active production GTM container was observed and no GTM Preview/container access was available.
GA4 property admin settings and key-event configuration could not be inspected from repository/network evidence.
No production RFQ was submitted; RFQ conversion behavior is based on source-code evidence and active direct GA4 availability.
activation_verdict: Decision B — Direct GA4 is active but GTM is not active.
recommended_next_action: Proceed to Phase 1 only after deciding whether to keep direct GA4 temporarily or switch to GTM-managed GA4; when activating GTM, first prevent duplicate GA4 measurement by not firing a GTM GA4 page_view until the direct GA4 path is disabled or isolated.
